text:
give me a query selector for all input fields that have the autofocus attribute set Here's how to select all input fields with the autofocus attribute: ```css input[autofocus] ``` You can also use it in JavaScript like this: ```javascript document.querySelectorAll('input[autofocus]') ``` This will select any `` element that has the autofocus attribute, regardless of the attribute's value. It works even if the attribute is simply present without a value, like ``. I need to also query by `name="query"` attribute. To select input fields that have both the `autofocus` attribute and `name="query"`, you can use: ```css input[autofocus][name="query"] ``` Or in JavaScript: ```javascript document.querySelectorAll('input[autofocus][name="query"]') ``` This will match elements like: ```html ``` The selector combines both attribute selectors to find elements that satisfy both conditions. ```html cloud/cloud/fieldsales/jinja2/fieldsales/agent_calendar_event_list.html {% extends "fieldsales/base.html" %} {% from "lists.html" import list_header, list_table %} {% from "datetimes.html" import date, timesince, datetime %} {% from "macros.html" import admin_link, bool_icon, user_badge, hover_helper, flag, icon %} {% from "users/macros.html" import user_info %} {% from "fieldsales/macros.html" import event_status %} {% from 'spinner.html' import spinner %} {% set section_list_url = url('fieldsales:events') %} {% set section_title = "Scheduled home visits" %} {% block content %} {{ super() }} {% include 'fieldsales/home_visit_stats_hx.html' %} {% call list_table(info_text) %} {{ _('Market') }} {{ list_header(request, ordering, 'agent__user__email', _('Agent')) }} {{ list_header(request, ordering, 'interest__customer__name', _('Details')) }} {{ list_header(request, ordering, 'created_at', _('Created at')) }} {{ list_header(request, ordering, 'slot__startswith', _('Time')) }} {{ _('Google calendar links') }} {{ _('Sync status') }} {% for event in events %}
{{ flag(event.location.country) }} {{ event.location.country.display }}
{{ user_badge(event.agent.user,with_fullname=True) }} {{ event.interest.customer.name }} {% for line in event.location.address_lines[:2] %} {{ line }} {% endfor %} {{ datetime(event.created_at) }} {{ timesince(event.created_at) }} {{ event.slot_as_str(event.agent.timezone) }} Estimated travel: {{ event.readable_travel_time }}
{% for title, link in event.google_event_links.items() %} {% if link %}{{ title }}{% endif %} {% endfor %} div> {% if hubspot_enabled %}
{{ _("HubSpot") }}: {{ event_status(id=event.hubspot_meeting_id) }}
{% endif %} {% if fetch_google_status %}
{{ _("Travel") }}: {{ event_status(id=event.travel_time_calendar_event_id) }}
{{ _("Home visit") }}: {{ event_status(id=event.appointment_calendar_event_id) }}
{% endif %} {% else %} {{ _('No events found') }} {% endfor %} {% endcall %} {% include "numbered_pagination_partial.html" %}

{% endblock content %} ``` I want to include google calendar favicons. their url structure looks like this `https://calendar.google.com/googlecalendar/images/favicons_2020q4/calendar_7.ico` please use `self.slot.lower` to construct the correct URL Based on the template, I'll help add Google Calendar favicons next to the calendar links. We can add an icon before each calendar link in the calendar links column. Here's how to modify the relevant section: ```html
{% for title, link in event.google_event_links.items() %} {% if link %}
{{ title }}
{% endif %} {% endfor %}
``` This will: 1. Add a container with flexbox for each link 2. Show the Google Calendar favicon using the hour from the event slot 3. Place the link next to the favicon with some spacing 4. Keep the existing link functionality The icons will change based on the hour of the event, which is extracted from `event.slot.lower.hour`. the _7 is todays date and we need to update
